mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of triggered instances or the t-statistic for return difference falls below 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth periods yield negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during disinflationary periods.
failure_condition: 20-day returns during disinflation are not significantly higher than baseline or Rank IC falls below 0.04 in rolling 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 3.5 percent year-over-year, equity markets anticipate restrictive monetary policy adjustments and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of high-inflation observations or predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly payroll gains and declining unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, which sustains wage growth and consumer demand, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences or the interaction effect fails to improve model fit with an increased AIC score.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue potential, mechanically lifting S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns following positive GDP prints are negative in more than 55% of cases or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side bottlenecks and impending earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day returns during tightening cycles with falling production are positive in more than 60% of instances or predictive power vanishes in walk-forward tests.
